model.label: DeepSeek V3.2
model.description: DeepSeek • General-purpose V-series model optimized for reasoning, coding, and efficient inference.

pick.rationale: Saint Mary's superior defensive efficiency, ranked 22nd nationally, should limit Texas A&M's offense and control the game's tempo. The Gaels' higher overall efficiency ratings and disciplined style provide a tangible edge in this 7-10 matchup. While Texas A&M's SEC experience is a factor, Saint Mary's defensive strength and team cohesion make them the more reliable pick.

reasoningStep.title: Efficiency Ratings and Defensive Advantage
reasoningStep.summary: Saint Mary's holds a clear advantage in defensive efficiency and overall adjusted efficiency margin, which is a strong predictor of tournament success. Texas A&M, while competitive, ranks lower in both offensive and defensive efficiency and faces a disciplined opponent.
evidence: Saint Mary's ranks 25th in Torvik and 24th in KenPom, while Texas A&M ranks 39th in both.
Saint Mary's defensive efficiency (adjDE 97.55) ranks 22nd, significantly better than Texas A&M's (adjDE 101.33, rank 43).
Saint Mary's adjusted efficiency margin (23.11) is 4.46 points better than Texas A&M's (18.65).
Saint Mary's record (27-5) indicates strong performance, even if against a slightly weaker schedule than Texas A&M's (21-11 in the SEC).

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.

modelTrace.content:
Search seems broken. I'll have to rely on ratings and general knowledge. I need to decide winner and confidence. Saint Mary's appears stronger by ratings, but Texas A&M's SEC schedule might prepare them better. However, Saint Mary's has played a tough non-conference schedule typically. Their ratings already account for schedule strength. Saint Mary's is ranked 24/25, Texas A&M 39. That's a decent gap but not huge. 7-10 games are often close. I should consider that Saint Mary's is a popular upset pick? Not necessarily. Usually 10-seeds win about 40% of the time historically.

I'll think about the matchup: Saint Mary's defense vs Texas A&M offense. Texas A&M's offense rank 41 (Torvik) vs Saint Mary's defense rank 22. That's advantage Saint Mary's. Saint Mary's offense rank 48 vs Texas A&M defense rank 43. Slight edge to Texas A&M defense. So Saint Mary's defense may be the key. They could slow down Texas A&M and grind out a win.

But Texas A&M's SEC experience might help them handle pressure. However, Saint Mary's is tournament-tested; they've made the tournament many times under Bennett.

I need to check if there are any known injuries for either team. Since search isn't working, I can't get latest news. But I can assume both are relatively healthy unless otherwise indicated. Usually for bracket predictions, we assume health unless news says otherwise.

Given the ratings, I'd lean Saint Mary's with Medium to High confidence. However, I should consider upset potential: Texas A&M's defense could stifle Saint Mary's offense, and if they get hot from three, they could win. But Saint Mary's is more efficient overall.

Let me look at the efficiency margin: Saint Mary's adjEM 23.11, Texas A&M 18.65. That's about 4.5 points per 100 possessions advantage for Saint Mary's. That's significant but not overwhelming.

I'll pick Saint Mary's with Medium confidence. But let me also consider that Texas A&M might have a coaching edge? Buzz Williams is a good tournament coach. Randy Bennett also has a good tournament record, though not deep runs. Both are solid.

I'll finalize: Saint Mary's.

Now I need to produce JSON with pick and reasoningStep. The reasoningStep should include evidence from ratings.

Let me create the JSON. First, pick object: gameId, winnerId (saint-mary-s), confidence (Medium), rationale (short paragraph). Then reasoningStep: id (maybe "analysis-1"), title, summary, evidence (list of key points). I'll use the ratings as evidence.

I'll write rationale: Saint Mary's superior defensive efficiency and overall team quality should give them the edge against Texas A&M. The Gaels' disciplined style and experience in close games will help them navigate a tough matchup, while the Aggies' offensive inconsistencies may prove costly against a top-25 defense.

Now reasoningStep: I'll summarize my analysis and include the ratings numbers.

Let me produce final JSON.
